blocks:
Surah Al-Fath (The Victory)

Medinan. Its number of verses is twenty-nine verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) إِنَّا فَتَحْنا لَكَ فَتْحاً مُبِيناً. (1) Indeed, We have granted you a manifest victory.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "When this verse was revealed, a verse was revealed to me that is dearer to me than the world and everything in it."

And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: The reason for the revelation of this Surah and this great victory is that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded His Messenger in a dream to enter the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), perform the circumambulation (الطواف), and shave his head with those who shave (المحلّقين). So he informed his companions and commanded them to depart, and they departed. When they reached Dhu al-Hulayfah, they entered the state of Ihrām (الإحرام) for Umrah (العمرة) and drove the sacrificial animals (البُدن). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم drove sixty-six sacrificial animals and marked them (أشعرها) upon entering Ihrām. They entered Ihrām from Dhu al-Hulayfah, reciting the Talbiyah for Umrah. Those among them who drove the sacrificial offering (الهدي) marked them with signs (شعرات مجلّلات).

When this reached Quraysh, they sent Khalid ibn al-Walid with two hundred horsemen as an ambush (كميناً) to intercept the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He was confronting him on the mountains. When they were partway along the road, the time for the noon prayer (صلاة الظهر) arrived. Bilal called the Adhān, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم led the people in prayer. Khalid ibn al-Walid said: "If we had charged them while they were praying, we would have struck them, for they do not interrupt their prayer. But now another prayer will come to them, which is dearer to them than the light of their eyes. If they enter that prayer, we will raid them." Then Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with the prayer of fear (صلاة الخوف) in His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَإِذا كُنْتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ الصَّلاةَ (And when you are among them, and lead them in prayer) (the verse in Surah An-Nisa'). We have already written the account of the prayer of fear therein.

When it was the second day, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم descended upon Al-Hudaybiyah, which is on the edge of the Sacred Precinct (الحرم). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had been mobilizing the Bedouins (الأعراب) along his route to join him, but no one followed him. They were saying: "Does Muhammad and his companions hope to enter the Sacred Precinct when Quraysh attacked them in the heart of their homes and killed them? Muhammad and his companions will never return to Medina." When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arrived to enter Al-Hudaybiyah, Quraysh came out swearing by Al-Lāt and Al-Uzzā that they would not let the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم enter Mecca while a single eye among them blinked (وفيهم عين تطرف). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent word to them: "I have not come for war; I have only come to perform my rituals (مناسكي) and slaughter my sacrificial animals (بُدني), and I will leave the meat for you."

So they sent Urwah ibn Mas'ud al-Thaqafi, who was wise and intelligent. He is the one concerning whom Allah revealed: **وَقالُوا لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْقَرْيَتَيْنِ عَظِيمٍ ** (And they say, 'Why was not this Qur’ān sent down to some great man from the two cities?')

When he approached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he magnified the matter and said: "O Muhammad, you have left your people, and they have pitched tents and brought out the milch camels (العود المطافيل), swearing by Al-Lāt and Al-Uzzā that they will not let you enter Mecca and their Sacred Precinct while a single eye among them blinks. Do you wish to destroy your family and your people, O Muhammad?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I have not come for war; I have only come to perform my rituals (مناسكي) and slaughter my sacrificial animals (بُدني), and I will leave the meat for you." Urwah said: "By Allah, I have never seen anyone repelled today as you have been repelled." So he returned to Quraysh and informed them. Quraysh said: "By Allah, if Muhammad enters Mecca and the Arabs hear about it, we will surely be humiliated, and the Arabs will dare to attack us."

So they sent Hafs ibn al-Ahnaf and Suhayl ibn Amr. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at them, He said: "Woe to Quraysh! War has exhausted them. Why do they not leave me alone with the Arabs? If I am truthful, I will only bring sovereignty to them along with the prophethood. And if I am a liar, the wolves (ذؤبان) of the Arabs will suffice them. No man from Quraysh asks me today for a plan (خطّة) in which there is no displeasure for Allah except that I will agree to it."

When they met the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, they said: "O Muhammad, why do you not return from us this year until we see what becomes of your affair and the affair of the Arabs? For the Arabs have heard about your journey, and if you enter our land and our Sacred Precinct, the Arabs will humiliate us and dare to attack us. We will leave the House (البيت) for you next year in this month for three days so that you may perform your rituals (نسكك) and depart from us." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم agreed to that. They said to Him: "You must return to us every man of ours who comes to you, and we will return to you every man of yours who comes to us." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "We have no need for any man of ours who comes to you. But [the condition is] that the Muslims in Mecca are not harmed in their declaration of Islam, nor are they coerced, nor is anything they do from the laws of Islam (شرائع الإسلام) denied to them." They accepted that.

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم agreed to the peace treaty (الصلح), most of his companions objected, and the strongest objection came from Umar. He said: "O Messenger of Allah, are we not upon the truth and our enemy upon falsehood?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Then should we grant humiliation (الذلّة) in our religion?" He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has promised me, and He will not break His promise." [Umar] said: "If I had forty men with me, I would have opposed Him." Suhayl ibn Amr and Hafs ibn al-Ahnaf returned to Quraysh and informed them of the peace treaty. Umar said: "O Messenger of Allah, did you not tell us that we would enter the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) and shave our heads with those who shave (المحلقين)?" He said: "Did I promise you this year? I told you that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has promised me that I will conquer Mecca, perform the circumambulation (أطوف), perform the running (أسعى), and shave my head with those who shave." When they pressed Him excessively, He said to them: "If you do not accept the peace treaty, then fight them." So they passed toward Quraysh, who were ready for war, and they charged them. The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were disgracefully defeated (هزيمة قبيحة) and passed by the Messenger of Allah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم smiled, then said: "O Ali, take the sword and face Quraysh." So the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام took his sword and charged Quraysh. When they looked at the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, they retreated. Then they said: "O Ali, has Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله changed His mind about what He granted us?" He said: "No." The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned, feeling ashamed, and began apologizing to the Messenger of Allah.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Were you not My companions on the Day of Badr, when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning you: إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ رَبَّكُمْ فَاسْتَجابَ لَكُمْ أَنِّي مُمِدُّكُمْ بِأَلْفٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ ** (When you pleaded with your Lord for help, He answered you: 'I will aid you with a thousand angels in a file')? Were you not My companions on the Day of Uhud: إِذْ تُصْعِدُونَ وَلا تَلْوُونَ عَلى أَحَدٍ وَالرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ فِي أُخْراكُمْ ** (When you were fleeing without casting a glance at anyone, while the Apostle was calling you from your rear)? Were you not My companions on the Day of such-and-such?" So they apologized to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and regretted what they had done, saying: "Allah and His Messenger know best, so do what seems right to you."

Hafs ibn al-Ahnaf and Suhayl ibn Amr returned to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, Quraysh has agreed to the condition regarding the declaration of Islam and that no one should be coerced regarding his religion." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called for the scribe (المكتب) and called the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام and told him to write: بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful). Suhayl ibn Amr said: "We do not recognize al-Rahmān (الرّحمن). Write as your fathers used to write: بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ (In Your Name, O Allah)." The Messenger of Allah said: "Write بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ (In Your Name, O Allah), for it is a Name among the Names of Allah. Then write: 'This is what Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the assembly (الملأ) of Quraysh have agreed upon.'"

Suhayl ibn Amr said: "If we knew that you were the Messenger of Allah, we would not have fought you. Write: 'This is what Muhammad ibn Abdullah has agreed upon.' Are you averse to your lineage, O Muhammad?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I am the Messenger of Allah, even if you do not acknowledge it." Then He said: "Erase it, O Ali, and write Muhammad ibn Abdullah." The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: "I will never erase your name from the prophethood (النبوّة)." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم erased it with His own hand. Then He wrote: "This is what Muhammad ibn Abdullah, the assembly (الملأ) of Quraysh, and Suhayl ibn Amr have agreed upon."

They agreed to cease war between them for ten years, on the condition that each side refrain from attacking the other, and that there be no secret raids (اسلال) nor treachery (أغلال), and that there be a restrained absence (غيبة مكفوفة) between them. And that whoever wishes to enter into the covenant (عهد) and agreement (عقد) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم may do so, and whoever wishes to enter into the covenant and agreement of Quraysh may do so. And that whoever comes to Muhammad without the permission of his guardian (وليه) shall be returned to him, but whoever comes to Quraysh from the companions of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم shall not be returned to Him. And that Islam shall be manifest in Mecca, and no one shall be coerced regarding his religion, nor harmed, nor reproached. And that Muhammad and His companions shall return from them this year, and then He shall enter Mecca next year and stay there for three days, and He shall not enter it with any weapon except the weapon of a traveler: swords in their sheaths (القراب). Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام wrote the document, and the Muhājirūn (المهاجرون) and the Ansār (الأنصار) witnessed the document.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Ali, you refused to erase My name from the prophethood. By the One Who sent Me with the truth as a Prophet, you will surely agree to a similar thing with their sons while you are distressed (مضيض) and oppressed (مضطهد)." When it was the Day of Siffin, and they agreed to the two arbitrators (الحكمين), [the scribe] wrote: "This is what the Commander of the Faithful Ali ibn Abi Talib and Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan have agreed upon." Amr ibn al-As said: "If we knew that you were the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, we would not have fought you. Rather, write: 'This is what Ali ibn Abi Talib and Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan have agreed upon.'" The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: "Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth; the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم informed me of that."

He said: When they wrote the document, Khuza'ah stood up and said: "We are in the covenant and agreement of Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah." And Banu Bakr stood up and said: "We are in the covenant and agreement of Quraysh." They wrote two copies: one copy with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and one copy with Suhayl ibn Amr.

Suhayl ibn Amr and Hafs ibn al-Ahnaf returned to Quraysh and informed them. The Messenger of Allah said to His companions: "Slaughter your sacrificial animals (بُدنكم) and shave your heads." They refused and said: "How can we slaughter and shave when we have not performed the circumambulation (الطواف) of the House (البيت) nor the running (السعي) between Safa and Marwah?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was distressed by this and complained about it to Umm Salamah. She said: "O Messenger of Allah, slaughter and shave yourself." So the people slaughtered, despite their certainty, doubt, and suspicion (على حيث يقين وشكّ وارتياب).

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said, magnifying the sacrificial animals (البُدن): "May Allah have mercy upon those who shave their heads (المحلّقين)." Some people said: "O Messenger of Allah, and those who shorten their hair (المقصّرين), because whoever did not drive a sacrificial offering (هدي) is not obligated to shave." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said a second time: "May Allah have mercy upon those who shave their heads, those who did not drive the sacrificial offering." They said: "O Messenger of Allah, and those who shorten their hair?" He said: "May Allah have mercy upon those who shorten their hair (المقصّرين)." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed toward Medina to Al-Na'im (النعيم) and rested beneath the tree. His companions who had objected to the peace treaty came to Him, apologized, and showed remorse for what they had done, asking the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to seek forgiveness for them. Then the Verse of Acceptance (آية الرضوان) was revealed.

I say: This story is mentioned in Rawdat al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, with additions and omissions. Whoever desires it should refer to it.

(2) لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللهُ ما تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَما تَأَخَّرَ ** (so that Allah may forgive you your past and future sins) This is the reason (علّة) for the victory (الفتح), in that it is caused by the struggle (جهاد) against the disbelievers, the effort to remove polytheism (الشرك), the exaltation of the religion (الدين), and the forceful perfection of deficient souls, so that this gradually becomes a trial (اختباراً) and a deliverance of the weak (الضعفة) from the hands of the oppressors (الظلمة).

In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "He did not have a sin, nor did He intend a sin, but Allah burdened Him with the sins of His followers (شيعته) and then forgave them for Him."

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: "By Allah, He did not have a sin, but Allah, the Glorified, guaranteed Him that He would forgive the sins of the followers (شيعة) of Ali (peace be upon him), both their past sins and their future sins."

Some of the people of knowledge (أهل المعرفة) said: His infallibility (عصمته) صلى الله عليه وآله has been established, so He has no sin. Therefore, the attribution of sin to Him remains only in the sense that He is the one addressed, but the intended meaning is His community (أمّته), just as it is said: "I address you, but listen, O neighbor." He said: "Your past sins (ما تقدم من ذنبك) are those from Adam until His time, and your future sins (وما تأخر) are those from His time until the Day of Resurrection, for all of them are His community (أمّته). There is no community except that it is under the Law (شرع) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, by virtue of the esoteric Name (اسم الباطن), since He was a Prophet while Adam was between water and clay. He is the Master of the Prophets and the Messengers, for He is the Master of mankind. Thus, Allah the Almighty gave glad tidings to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله with His saying: لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللهُ ما تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَما تَأَخَّرَ ** (so that Allah may forgive you your past and future sins) due to the universality of His message to all mankind, and what is incumbent upon people is not merely seeing His person. Just as He directed His Messenger, Ali (peace be upon him), to Yemen during the time of His manifestation to convey the call (الدعوة), similarly, He directed the Messengers and Prophets to their communities from the time He was a Prophet while Adam was between water and clay. So, He called everyone to Allah, and everyone is His community (أمّته) from Adam until the Day of Resurrection. Thus, Allah gave Him glad tidings of forgiveness for the past sins of mankind and their future sins, and He was the one addressed, but mankind was the intended meaning. So, He forgives all and grants them happiness. This is fitting for the universality of His Mercy, which encompasses all things, and for the universality of the rank of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, since He was sent to all mankind explicitly (بالنص). He did not say, "We sent you specifically to this community," but rather informed that He is a Messenger sent to all mankind (الناس كافة). And mankind, from Adam (peace be upon him) until the Day of Resurrection, are the ones intended by the address of Allah's forgiveness for His past sin and His future sin.

I say: That which conveys this meaning has already passed in the Third Introduction (المقدّمة الثالثة).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: He was asked about this Ayah, and he replied: "No one was considered a greater sinner by the polytheists of Mecca than the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, because they used to worship three hundred and sixty idols besides Allah. When He came to them with the call (الدعوة) to the word of sincerity (كلمة الإخلاص), this was grievous and immense to them, and they said: أَجَعَلَ الْآلِهَةَ إِلهاً واحِداً ** (Has he reduced the gods to one God?) up to His saying: إِلَّا اخْتِلاقٌ ** (This is nothing but a fabrication.) When Allah the Almighty granted His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله the victory of Mecca, He the Almighty said: 'O Muhammad, إِنَّا فَتَحْنا لَكَ فَتْحاً مُبِيناً (Indeed, We have granted you a manifest victory) لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللهُ ما تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَما تَأَخَّرَ (so that Allah may forgive you your past and future sins)' in the view of the polytheists of Mecca, due to your call to the Oneness of Allah (توحيد الله) regarding what preceded and what followed. This is because some of the polytheists of Mecca embraced Islam, and some left Mecca, and those who remained were unable to deny the Oneness of Allah against Him when He called people to it. Thus, His sin became forgiven in their view due to His triumph over them."

And in the narration of Ibn Tawus from them: The intended meaning of ** لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللهُ ما تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَما تَأَخَّرَ ** (so that Allah may forgive you your past and future sins) is in the view of the people of Mecca and Quraysh—meaning what preceded the Hijra and what followed it. For when you conquer Mecca without killing them, or eradicating them, or taking them to task for the enmity and fighting they committed, they will forgive what they used to believe was a sin of yours in their view, whether past or future, and what appeared of His enmity toward them in response to their enmity toward Him. When they saw that He had gained control and power, yet did not seek retribution (استقصى), they forgave the sins they had assumed. وَيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكَ ** (and complete His blessing upon you) by exalting the religion (الدين) and joining sovereignty (الملك) to prophethood (النبوّة). وَيَهْدِيَكَ صِراطاً مُسْتَقِيماً (and guide you on a straight path) in conveying the message (الرسالة) and establishing the rites of leadership (الرياسة).

(٣) وَيَنْصُرَكَ اللهُ نَصْراً عَزِيزاً ** (3) And that Allah may help you with a mighty help. A help (نصراً) in which there is might (عزّ) and invincibility (منعة). (٤) هُوَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ ** (4) It is He who sent down tranquility Steadfastness (الثبات) and reassurance (الطّمأنينة). In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It is faith (الايمان). ** فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** into the hearts of the faithful Al-Qummi (said): They are those who did not oppose the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and did not deny the peace treaty (الصلح) to Him. لِيَزْدادُوا إِيماناً مَعَ إِيمانِهِمْ that they might increase in faith along with their faith— certainty (يقيناً) along with their certainty (يقينهم) through the firmness of the creed (العقيدة) and the soul's reassurance (اطمئنان النفس) concerning it. Or, that they might increase in faith (ايماناً) in the Divine Laws (الشرائع) along with their faith in Allah and the Last Day. And the discussion regarding the increase of faith has already passed at the end of Surat al-Tawbah. وَلِلَّهِ جُنُودُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ and to Allah belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth, He manages their affair, sometimes giving some of them dominance over others, and sometimes establishing peace among them, as His wisdom (حكمته) necessitates. وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً ** and Allah is all-knowing, of the interests (المصالح). حَكِيماً ** all-wise. in what He decrees (يقدّر) and manages (يدبّر).

(٥) ** لِيُدْخِلَ ** He did what He did and managed what He managed ** لِيُدْخِلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَيُكَفِّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ ** (5) that He may admit the faithful, men and women, into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever, and absolve them of their misdeeds. He covers them and does not reveal them. وَكانَ ذلِكَ عِنْدَ اللهِ فَوْزاً عَظِيماً (And that is a great triumph with Allah) because it is the ultimate limit of what is sought, whether it be attracting benefit or repelling harm.

(٦) وَيُعَذِّبَ الْمُنافِقِينَ وَالْمُنافِقاتِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ وَالْمُشْرِكاتِ الظَّانِّينَ بِاللهِ ظَنَّ السَّوْءِ ** (6) and punish the hypocritical men and hypocritical women, and the polytheistic men and polytheistic women, who entertain evil thoughts about Allah. And that is that He will not grant victory to His Messenger and the believers. عَلَيْهِمْ دائِرَةُ السَّوْءِ ** upon them shall be the turn of evil. The turn (دائرة) of what they imagine and anticipate for the believers will not bypass them. And السُّوء was read with a ḍammah (بالضمّ). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) (said): And they are those who denied the peace treaty (الصلح) and accused the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. وَغَضِبَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَعَنَهُمْ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ جَهَنَّمَ وَساءَتْ مَصِيراً. and Allah is wrathful at them, and has cursed them, and prepared for them hell, and it is an evil destination.

(٧) وَلِلَّهِ جُنُودُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَكانَ اللهُ عَزِيزاً حَكِيماً. (7) And to Allah belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (٨) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ شاهِداً على امّتك وَمُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً على الطاعة والمعصية (8) We have surely sent you as a witness, and a bearer of good news and a warner, ** إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ شاهِداً ** over your nation وَمُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً ** regarding obedience and disobedience. (٩) لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُعَزِّرُوهُ وتقوّوه بتقوية دينه ورسوله وَتُوَقِّرُوهُ وتعظموه وَتُسَبِّحُوهُ وتنزّهوه بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً غدوة وعشيّاً وقرأ الأربعة بالياء. (9) so that you may have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and support Him, and revere Him, and glorify Him morning and evening. ** لِتُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُعَزِّرُوهُ ** and strengthen Him by strengthening His religion and His Apostle, وَتُوَقِّرُوهُ and magnify Him وَتُسَبِّحُوهُ ** and declare Him free from imperfection بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً (morning and evening) [meaning] in the morning (غدوة) and the evening (عشيّاً). The four [Imams/Reciters] read [the verbs] with the letter yā (بالياء). (١٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبايِعُونَكَ إِنَّما يُبايِعُونَ اللهَ لأنّه المقصود ببيعته يَدُ اللهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ يعني يدك التي فوق أيديهم في حال بيعتهم ايّاك انّما هي بمنزلة يد الله لأنّهم في الحقيقة يبايعون الله عزّ وجلّ ببيعتك. (10) Indeed those who swear fealty to you, swear fealty to Allah. The hand of Allah is over their hands. ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبايِعُونَكَ إِنَّما يُبايِعُونَ اللهَ ** because He is the intended object of their fealty (بيعته). يَدُ اللهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** meaning, your hand which is over their hands during their swearing of fealty to you is only in the position of the Hand of Allah, because in reality, they are swearing fealty to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), through their fealty to you. وفي العيون عن الرضا عليه السلام في حديث : بيعة الناس له قال عقد البيعة هو من على الخنصر الى على الإبهام وفسخها من اعلى الإبهام الى اعلى الخنصر وفي إرشاد المفيد في حديث : بيعتهم له قال فرفع الرضا عليه السلام يده فتلقى بها وجهه وببطنها وجوههم فقال له المأمون ابسط يدك للبيعة فقال الرضا عليه السلام انّ رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله هكذا كان يبايع فبايعه الناس ويده فوق أيديهم فَمَنْ نَكَثَ نقض العهد فَإِنَّما يَنْكُثُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ فلا يعود ضرر نكثه الّا عليه وَمَنْ أَوْفى بِما عاهَدَ عَلَيْهُ اللهَ وفي الكافي : مبايعته فَسَيُؤْتِيهِ أَجْراً عَظِيماً وهو الجنّة In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a hadith concerning the people's fealty to him, he said: "The binding of the fealty (عقد البيعة) is from the top of the little finger (الخنصر) to the top of the thumb (الإبهام), and its dissolution (فسخها) is from the top of the thumb to the top of the little finger." And in Irshad al-Mufid, in a hadith concerning their fealty to him, he said: So Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) raised His hand and received their faces with it, and their faces with its palm. Al-Ma'mun said to Him: "Extend your hand for the fealty." Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to swear fealty in this manner, so the people swore fealty to Him while His hand was above their hands." ** فَمَنْ نَكَثَ ** [meaning] broke the covenant (نقض العهد) فَإِنَّما يَنْكُثُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ ** so the harm of his violation returns only upon himself. وَمَنْ أَوْفى بِما عاهَدَ عَلَيْهُ اللهَ (and whoever fulfills the covenant he has made with Allah)—and in al-Kafi: [it reads] His fealty (مبايعته)— فَسَيُؤْتِيهِ أَجْراً عَظِيماً ** which is Paradise (الجنّة). وقرئ عليه بضمّ الهاء فسنؤتيه بالنون القمّيّ نزلت في بيعة الرضوان لَقَدْ رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ واشترط عليهم ان لا ينكروا بعد ذلك على رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله شيئاً يفعله ولا يخالفوه في شيء يأمرهم به فقال الله عزّ وجلّ بعد نزول آية الرضوان إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبايِعُونَكَ إِنَّما يُبايِعُونَ اللهَ يَدُ اللهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ الآية وانّما رضي الله عنهم بهذا الشرط ان يفوا بعد ذلك بعهد الله وميثاقه ولا ينقضوا عهده وعقده فبهذا العقد رضي الله عنهم فقدّموا في التأليف آية الشرط على آية الرضوان وانّما نزلت اوّلاً بيعة الرضوان ثمّ آية الشرط عليهم فيها. It was read [the pronoun عليه] with the ḍammah on the hā (بضمّ الهاء), and [it was read] fa-sa-nu'tīhi (فسنؤتيه) with the nūn (بالنون). Al-Qummi [said]: It was revealed concerning the Pledge of al-Riḍwān (بيعة الرضوان). لَقَدْ رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ ** (7) Allah was certainly pleased with the faithful when they swore fealty to you under the tree. And He stipulated to them that they should not thereafter deny anything the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did, nor oppose Him in anything He commanded them. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), said after the revelation of the verse of al-Riḍwān: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبايِعُونَكَ إِنَّما يُبايِعُونَ اللهَ يَدُ اللهِ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (10) Indeed those who swear fealty to you, swear fealty to Allah. The hand of Allah is over their hands. the Ayah. And He was only pleased with them on account of this condition: that they would thereafter fulfill the covenant (عهد) and compact (ميثاق) of Allah, and not break His covenant (عهد) and His contract (عقد). So, it was by this contract (العقد) that Allah was pleased with them. They placed the verse of the condition (آية الشرط) before the verse of al-Riḍwān in the compilation (التأليف), whereas the Pledge of al-Riḍwān was revealed first, and then the verse of the condition concerning it was revealed to them.

(١١) سَيَقُولُ لَكَ الْمُخَلَّفُونَ مِنَ الْأَعْرابِ ** (The stay-at-homes from the Bedouins will say to you) It was said that they are the tribes of Aslam, Juhaynah, Muzaynah, and Ghifar. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called them to arms in the year of al-Hudaybiyyah, but they stayed behind and excused themselves by claiming preoccupation with their wealth and families. However, what truly kept them behind was abandonment (الخذلان), weakness of faith (ضعف العقيدة), and fear of fighting Quraysh should they block their path.

And al-Qummi said: They are those whom He called to arms during al-Hudaybiyyah. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned to Medina from al-Hudaybiyyah, He raided Khaybar. The stay-at-homes sought His permission to go out with Him. So Allah the Almighty said: سَيَقُولُ الْمُخَلَّفُونَ إِذَا انْطَلَقْتُمْ ** (The stay-at-homes will say when you set out) up to His saying إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (except a few): شَغَلَتْنا أَمْوالُنا وَأَهْلُونا (Our possessions and our families kept us busy), since we had no one to manage their affairs for us. فَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَنا ** (so ask forgiveness for us) from Allah for staying behind. يَقُولُونَ ** (They say) with their tongues ما لَيْسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (what is not in their hearts), which is a refutation of their excuse and their seeking of forgiveness. قُلْ فَمَنْ يَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً (Say: 'Who can avail you anything against Allah?'), meaning: Who can prevent you from His will and His decree إِنْ أَرادَ بِكُمْ ضَرًّا ** (if He desires a harm for you) — that which harms you, such as death, defeat, damage to wealth and family, or punishment for staying behind? And it was read (وقرئ) with ḍammah (ضُرًّا). أَوْ أَرادَ بِكُمْ نَفْعاً (or desires a benefit for you) — that which contradicts the harm. بَلْ كانَ اللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيراً ** (Rather, Allah is All-aware of what you do), for He knows your staying behind and your intention therein.

(١٢) بَلْ ظَنَنْتُمْ أَنْ لَنْ يَنْقَلِبَ الرَّسُولُ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ إِلى أَهْلِيهِمْ أَبَداً ** (12) Nay, you thought that the Apostle and the faithful would never return to their families Due to your assumption that the polytheists would utterly destroy them. وَزُيِّنَ ذلِكَ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ** (and that was made fair-seeming in your hearts) So it became firmly established therein. وَظَنَنْتُمْ ظَنَّ السَّوْءِ وَكُنْتُمْ قَوْماً بُوراً ** (and you entertained an evil thought, and you were a ruinous people) Perishing in the sight of Allah due to the corruption of your belief (عقيدتكم) and the evil of your intention (نيّتكم). Al-Qummi said: Meaning, a people of evil (قوم سوء).

(١٣) وَمَنْ لَمْ يُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ فَإِنَّا أَعْتَدْنا لِلْكافِرِينَ سَعِيراً ** (13) And whoever does not believe in Allah and His Apostle, We have indeed prepared a blaze for the faithless. He drew attention to their disbelief, then confirmed the consequence by placing the explicit noun (الظاهر) in the place of the pronoun (الضمير). (١٤) وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (14) To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. He governs however He wills. يَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً ** He forgives whomever He wishes, and punishes whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. For forgiveness (الغفران) and mercy (الرّحمة) are among His habit (دأبه), while punishment (التعذيب) falls under His decree (قضائه) secondarily (بالعرض). And for that reason, it came in the Hadith Qudsi: "My mercy preceded My wrath."

(١٥) سَيَقُولُ الْمُخَلَّفُونَ ** (15) The stay-at-homes will say meaning those who were mentioned إِذَا انْطَلَقْتُمْ إِلى مَغانِمَ لِتَأْخُذُوها ** when you set out for the spoils to take them. meaning the spoils of Khaybar ذَرُونا نَتَّبِعْكُمْ يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُبَدِّلُوا كَلامَ اللهِ ** Leave us to follow you. They desire to change Allah’s word. that they may alter it, and it is His promise to the people of al-Hudaybiyyah that He would compensate them for the spoils of Mecca with the spoils of Khaybar. And it was recited (كَلِمَ اللهِ). قُلْ لَنْ تَتَّبِعُونا ** Say, ‘You shall never follow us.’ A negation (نَفْي) in the meaning of prohibition (نَهْي). كَذلِكُمْ قالَ اللهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** Thus has Allah said beforehand. before their preparation for setting out to Khaybar. فَسَيَقُولُونَ بَلْ تَحْسُدُونَنا Then they will say, ‘No, you are envious of us.’ that we should share the spoils with you. بَلْ كانُوا لا يَفْقَهُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** No, they understand nothing except a little. except a slight understanding, which is their shrewdness concerning worldly affairs.

(١٦) قُلْ لِلْمُخَلَّفِينَ مِنَ الْأَعْرابِ ** (16) Say to the laggards of the Bedouins: He repeated their mention by this name (الاسم) as an exaggeration in censure (الذمّ) and an indication of the ugliness (شناعة) of lagging behind (التخلّف). سَتُدْعَوْنَ إِلى قَوْمٍ أُولِي بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ you will be called to a people possessing great might: It is said they are Hawazin (هوازن) and Thaqif (ثقيف). ** تُقاتِلُونَهُمْ أَوْ يُسْلِمُونَ ** whom you will fight until they submit: Meaning, one of the two matters (الامرين) will occur. ** فَإِنْ تُطِيعُوا يُؤْتِكُمُ اللهُ أَجْراً حَسَناً ** If you obey, Allah will grant you a good reward: It is the spoils (الغنيمة) in this world (الدنيا) and Paradise (الجنّة) in the Hereafter (الآخرة). وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا كَما تَوَلَّيْتُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ But if you turn your backs, as you turned your backs before (referring to) al-Hudaybiyyah (الحديبية). ** يُعَذِّبْكُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً ** He will punish you with a painful punishment, due to the doubling (تضاعف) of your crime (جرمكم).

(١٧) لَيْسَ عَلَى الْأَعْمى حَرَجٌ وَلا عَلَى الْأَعْرَجِ حَرَجٌ وَلا عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ حَرَجٌ ** (17) There is no blame on the blind, nor is there any blame on the lame, nor is there any blame on the sick. When He warned against staying behind, He negated the blame (الحرج) from these excused ones, making them an exception from the threat (الوعيد). وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ ** And whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, He will admit him into gardens with streams running in them. And it was said: He detailed the promise (الوعد) and summarized the threat (الوعيد), emphasizing the promise due to the precedence of His mercy. Then He compensated for that by repetition by way of generalization, so He said: وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّ يُعَذِّبْهُ عَذاباً أَلِيماً ** But whoever turns away, He will punish him with a painful punishment. since intimidation (الترهيب) here is more beneficial than encouragement (الترغيب). And يُدْخِلْهُ and يُعَذِّبْهُ were read as نُدْخِلْهُ and نُعَذِّبْهُ with the ن (النون).

(١٨) ** لَقَدْ رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ ** (18) Indeed Allah was pleased with the faithful when they swore allegiance to you under the tree. Its story has already preceded. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: ‘Ali (peace be upon him) wrote to Mu'awiyah: "I am the first one who swore allegiance to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم under the tree, regarding His saying: لَقَدْ رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ يُبايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ (Indeed Allah was pleased with the faithful when they swore allegiance to you under the tree.) فَعَلِمَ ما فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ فَأَنْزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (So He knew what was in their hearts, and He sent down tranquility upon them,) Tranquility (الطمأنينة) and the calmness of the soul (سكون النفس). وَأَثابَهُمْ فَتْحاً قَرِيباً ** (and rewarded them with a victory near at hand,) The conquest of Khaybar (فتح خيبر) immediately after their departure.

(١٩) وَمَغانِمَ كَثِيرَةً يَأْخُذُونَها ** (19) And many spoils that they will take. meaning the spoils of Khaybar. وَكانَ اللهُ عَزِيزاً حَكِيماً ** And Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. Dominant (غالباً), observing the requirement of wisdom (مراعياً مقتضى الحكمة). (٢٠) وَعَدَكُمُ اللهُ مَغانِمَ كَثِيرَةً تَأْخُذُونَها ** (20) Allah has promised you many spoils that you will take, And these are what revert (ما يفيء) to the believers until the Day of Resurrection. فَعَجَّلَ لَكُمْ هذِهِ ** and He hastened this one for you, meaning the spoils of Khaybar. وَكَفَّ أَيْدِيَ النَّاسِ عَنْكُمْ ** and restrained people’s hands from you, the hands of the people of Khaybar and their allies. وَلِتَكُونَ آيَةً لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ and that it may be a sign for the faithful, a sign (امارة) by which they recognize the truthfulness of the Messenger in His promise to them. وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ صِراطاً مُسْتَقِيماً ** and that He may guide you to a straight path. which is confidence in the favor (فضل) of Allah and reliance (التوكل) upon Him.

(21) وَأُخْرى لَمْ تَقْدِرُوا after عَلَيْها قَدْ أَحاطَ اللهُ بِها وَكانَ اللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيراً. (21) and another [gain] which you have not yet been able to achieve. Allah has encompassed it, and Allah has power over all things. (22) وَلَوْ قاتَلَكُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا from the people of Mecca, and they did not make peace, they would have surely turned their backs, meaning: they would have been defeated, then they would have found no protector who guards them وَلا نَصِيراً who helps them. (22) Had the faithless fought you, they would have surely turned their backs, and then they would have found no protector nor helper. (23) سُنَّةَ اللهِ الَّتِي قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلُ meaning: the way of the triumph of His Prophets, an ancient way concerning those nations who passed away, just as He said: كَتَبَ اللهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي وَلَنْ تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ اللهِ تَبْدِيلاً alteration. (23) This is the way of Allah that has passed before, and you will never find any change in the way of Allah.

(٢٤) وَهُوَ الَّذِي كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنْكُمْ ** (24) It is He who restrained their hands from you The hands of the disbelievers of Mecca ** وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ بِبَطْنِ مَكَّةَ ** and your hands from them in the valley of Mecca, inside Mecca ** مِنْ بَعْدِ أَنْ أَظْفَرَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** after He had given you victory over them. Al-Qummi (said): meaning, after you headed (أَمَمْتُمْ) from Medina towards the Sacred Precinct (الحرم), and they requested peace (الصلح) from you, after they had been raiding you in Medina. They began requesting peace after you had been requesting peace from them. ** وَكانَ اللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيراً ** And Allah is watchful of what you do. [This refers to] your fighting them first, in obedience to His Messenger, and your restraining yourselves second, out of reverence for His House (بيته). And it was read with the yā' (بالياء).

(٢٥) هُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوكُمْ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ وَالْهَدْيَ مَعْكُوفاً ** (25) It is they who disbelieved and barred you from the Sacred Mosque, and the offering, detained, detained (محبوساً) from reaching its place (مَحِلَّهُ). The offering (الهدي) is what is presented to Mecca (مكّة), and its place (مَحِلَّهُ) is the location where its slaughter (نحره) is permissible (يحلّ) therein. وَلَوْ لا رِجالٌ مُؤْمِنُونَ وَنِساءٌ مُؤْمِناتٌ ** and were it not for faithful men and faithful women Al-Qummiyy said: meaning in Mecca (مكّة). لَمْ تَعْلَمُوهُمْ ** whom you do not know You did not recognize them individually (بأعيانهم) due to their mingling with the polytheists (بالمشركين). أَنْ تَطَؤُهُمْ ** lest you should trample them Meaning, lest you should fall upon them (تواقعوا بهم) and initiate (attack) against them (تبتدءوهم). فَتُصِيبَكُمْ مِنْهُمْ ** and thus incur on their account from their direction (من جهتهم) harm (مَعَرَّةٌ)—a disliked thing (مكروه)—such as the necessity of blood money (الدية) and expiation (الكفّارة) for killing them, regret over them, the reproach of the disbelievers concerning that, and sin (الإثم) due to negligence in searching for them. بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ** unknowingly. Meaning, you trample them without knowing them (عالمين بهم). The answer to لولا (were it not for) is omitted because the context indicates it. The meaning is: Were it not for the dislike of you destroying faithful people among the disbelievers, unknowingly, such that harm (مكروه) would befall you due to their destruction, He would not have restrained your hands from them.

Al-Qummiyy said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, informed His Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) that the reason (علّة) for the truce (الصلح) was only for the faithful men and faithful women who were in Mecca (مكّة). Had there been no truce, and had there been war, they would have been killed. But when the truce occurred, they became safe (آمنوا) and manifested Islam (أظهروا الإسلام). It is said that this truce was a greater victory (فتحاً) for the Muslims than their conquest. لِيُدْخِلَ اللهُ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ** so that Allah may admit into His mercy This is the reason (علّة) for what the restraining of hands from the people of Mecca indicated, protecting those among them who were faithful. Meaning, that was so that Allah may admit into His divine success (توفيقه) for the increase of goodness and Islam whom He wills, whether from their faithful or their polytheists. ** لَوْ تَزَيَّلُوا ** (if they had been separated) meaning, if they had separated (تفرّقوا) and distinguished themselves (تميّز) one from another, ** لَعَذَّبْنَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً ** (We would have punished the disbelievers among them with a painful punishment) by killing and captivity (بالقتل والسبي). Al-Qummiyy said: meaning these faithful men and faithful women who were in Mecca (مكّة), had they moved away from them and left their midst, لَعَذَّبْنَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ.** (We would have punished the disbelievers among them.)

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Was not ‘Ali (peace be upon him) strong in the command of Allah?" He said: "Yes, indeed." It was asked: "What prevented him from repelling [the enemy] or resisting?" He said: "You have asked, so understand the answer. ‘Ali (peace be upon him) was prevented from that by an Ayah from the Book of Allah, the Almighty." It was asked: "And which Ayah?" So he recited the Ayah: ** لَوْ تَزَيَّلُوا ** (if they had been separated). Indeed, Allah, the Almighty, had faithful trusts (ودائع) in the loins (أصلاب) of a people who were disbelievers and hypocrites. So ‘Ali (peace be upon him) would not kill the fathers until the trusts (الودائع) had emerged. When they emerged, he appeared against those against whom he appeared and killed them. And likewise, our Qā’im (قائمنا) of the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them) will never appear until the trusts (ودايع) of Allah emerge. When they emerge, he will appear against those against whom he appears and kill them.

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), is that which has the same meaning, through multiple chains of transmission (بأسانيد متعدّدة). Among them, he said regarding this Ayah: "Had Allah brought forth what was in the loins (أصلاب) of the faithful from the disbelievers, and what was in the loins (أصلاب) of the disbelievers from the faithful, ** لَعَذَّبْنَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (We would have punished the disbelievers)."

(٢٦) ** إِذْ جَعَلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْحَمِيَّةَ ** (26) When the faithless put the zealotry Arrogance (الأنفة) ** حَمِيَّةَ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ ** of the Age of Ignorance in their hearts. Which prevents submission to the truth (إذعان الحق). Al-Qummi means Quraysh and Suhayl ibn 'Amr, and when they said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "We do not know al-Rahman al-Rahim (الرحمن الرحيم)," and their saying: "If we knew that you were the Messenger of Allah, we would not have fought you, so write Muhammad ibn 'Abd Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." ** فَأَنْزَلَ اللهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ وَعَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Apostle and upon the faithful, He sent down upon them steadfastness (الثبات) and solemnity (الوقار), so they endured their zealotry (حميّتهم). ** وَأَلْزَمَهُمْ كَلِمَةَ التَّقْوى ** and made them stick to the word of Godwariness. The word of testimony (كلمة الشهادة).

In al-Qummi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, that He said in His sermon: "And the most worthy of speech is the word of Godwariness (كلمة التقوى)."

And in al-Ilal, narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله, that He said: "In the interpretation of Lā ilāha illā Allāh (لا اله الّا الله), which is the word of Godwariness (كلمة التقوى), Allah accepts the scales (الموازين) by it on the Day of Resurrection."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, that He was asked about it, and He said: "It is faith (الإيمان)."

And in al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: "Indeed, 'Ali is the banner of guidance (راية الهدى), the Imam of My allies (أوليائي), and the light for whoever obeys Me. And He is the word (الكلمة) which I have made incumbent upon the Godwary (المتّقين)."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from Him عليه السلام, He said in a sermon: "We are the word of Godwariness (كلمة التقوى) and the path of guidance (سبيل الهدى)."

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, He said in a sermon: "I am the firmest handle of Allah (عروة الله الوثقى) and the word of Godwariness (الكلمة التقوى)."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Rida عليه السلام, in a narration of His: "We are the word of Godwariness (كلمة التقوى) and the firmest handle (العروة الوثقى). وَكانُوا أَحَقَّ بِها وَأَهْلَها (and they were worthier of it and entitled to it) and the deserving ones (المستأهل) of it."

** وَكانَ اللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيماً ** and Allah is all-knowing about all things. So He knows the people (اهل) of every thing and makes it easy (ييسرّه) for them.

(٢٧) لَقَدْ صَدَقَ اللهُ رَسُولَهُ الرُّؤْيا ** He confirmed him in his vision. بِالْحَقِ ** being invested with it, for what He saw was inevitably at its appointed time. Its story has already preceded at the beginning of the Surah. لَتَدْخُلُنَّ الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرامَ إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ آمِنِينَ مُحَلِّقِينَ رُؤُسَكُمْ وَمُقَصِّرِينَ ** (27) Certainly Allah has fulfilled His Apostle’s vision: (You will surely enter the Sacred Mosque, if Allah wills, in safety, with your heads shaven or hair cut short, without any fear.) some of you shaving (مُحَلِّقاً) and others cutting short (مُقَصِّراً), لا تَخافُونَ ** after that. فَعَلِمَ ما لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا ** (He knew what you did not know,) of the wisdom in delaying that, فَجَعَلَ مِنْ دُونِ ذلِكَ فَتْحاً قَرِيباً ** (and appointed besides that a victory near at hand.) This is the conquest of Khaybar (فَتْحُ خَيْبَر), so that the hearts of the believers might find comfort in it until the promised event is facilitated.

(٢٨) ** هُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالْهُدى وَدِينِ الْحَقِ ** (28) He it is who sent His Apostle with the guidance and the religion of the truth وبدين الإسلام لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ ليغلبه على جنس الدين كلّه بنسخ ما كان حقّاً وإظهار فساد ما كان باطلاً ثمّ بتسليط المسلمين على اهله إذ ما أهل دين إلّا وقد قهر بالإسلام أو سيقهر وفيه تأكيد لما وعده بالفتح. and with the religion of Islam, that He may make it prevail over all religion (لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ), so that He may make it dominant over the entire genus of religion, by abrogating what was true and revealing the corruption of what was false, and then by granting the Muslims dominance over its adherents, since there is no follower of a religion except that he has been subdued by Islam or will be subdued. In this, there is confirmation of what He promised him regarding the conquest (الفتح).

القمّيّ : هو الإمام عليه السلام الذي يظهره الله عزّ وجلّ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ* فيملأ الأرض قسطاً وعدلاً كما ملئت ظلماً وجوراً قال وهذا ممّا ذكرنا انّ تأويله بعد تنزيله. Al-Qummi said: He is the Imam (peace be upon him) whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will make prevail over all religion (عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ), so that He will fill the earth with equity and justice, just as it was filled with oppression and tyranny. He said: And this is among what we mentioned, that its interpretation (تأويله) comes after its revelation (تنزيله).

أقول : قد سبق تمام الكلام فيه في سورة التوبة I say: The complete discussion regarding this has already preceded in Surat al-Tawbah.

وَكَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً ** and Allah suffices as a witness. على انّ ما وعده كائن أو على رسالته. regarding the fact that what He promised is inevitable, or regarding His apostleship (رسالته).

(29) ** مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللهِ ** (Muhammad is the Apostle of Allah) This sentence clarifies what is testified to (المشهود به), or it is a new introductory sentence (استيناف) along with what is conjoined to it. Following them is the predicate (خبر) وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ أَشِدَّاءُ عَلَى الْكُفَّارِ رُحَماءُ بَيْنَهُمْ (and those who are with him are hard against the faithless, merciful among themselves). They are harsh towards those who oppose their religion and show mercy among themselves, like His saying: ** أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ ** (humble towards the faithful, stern towards the faithless). ** تَراهُمْ رُكَّعاً سُجَّداً ** (You see them bowing and prostrating) because they are occupied with prayer during most of their times. ** يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلاً مِنَ اللهِ وَرِضْواناً ** (seeking grace from Allah and [His] pleasure)—meaning reward (الثواب) and pleasure (الرضا). ** سِيماهُمْ (1) فِي وُجُوهِهِمْ مِنْ أَثَرِ السُّجُودِ ** (Their mark is in their faces, the trace of prostration). It was said that He means the sign (السمة) that appears on their foreheads due to the abundance of prostration. In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: "It is staying up late (السهر) in prayer." ** ذلِكَ مَثَلُهُمْ فِي التَّوْراةِ ** (That is their parable in the Torah)—meaning their description (صفتهم) of wondrous import mentioned therein. وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِي الْإِنْجِيلِ (and their parable in the Evangel).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: This verse was revealed concerning the Jews and the Christians: ** الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يَعْرِفُونَهُ كَما يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْناءَهُمْ ** (those whom We have given the Book recognize Him just as they recognize their own sons)—meaning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had revealed in the Torah, the Evangel, and the Psalms (الزبور) the description (صفة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, his mission (مبعثه), and his migration (مهاجره). This is His saying: ** مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللهِ ** صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم up to His saying ** فِي الْإِنْجِيلِ . This is His description in the Torah and the Evangel, and the description of His companions. So when Allah sent Him, the People of the Book recognized Him, just as He, majestic is His glory, said: ** كَزَرْعٍ أَخْرَجَ شَطْأَهُ ** (like a crop which puts forth its shoots)—meaning its sprouts (فراخه). And it was recited (وقرئ) with fathah on all letters (بالفتحات). ** فَآزَرَهُ ** (and strengthens it)—meaning He strengthened it (فقواه), derived from al-mu'āzarah (الموازرة), which is mutual assistance (المعاونة), or from al-īzār (الايزار), which is assistance (الاعانة). And it was recited (وقرئ) fa'āzarahu (فأزره) like ajirahu (اجره) in ājirahu (آجره). فَاسْتَغْلَظَ (then it becomes thick)—meaning it changed from thinness (الدقة) to thickness (الغلظ). فَاسْتَوى عَلى سُوقِهِ (and stands firm on its stalks)—meaning it became straight upon its stems (قصبه), which is the plural of sāq (ساق). And it was recited (وقرئ) su'uqih (سؤقه) with a hamzah (بالهمزة). ** يُعْجِبُ الزُّرَّاعَ ** (delighting the sowers) due to its density (بكثافته), strength (وقوته), thickness (وغلظه), and beautiful appearance (وحسن منظره). It was said that this is a parable Allah struck for the Companions (الصحابة). They were few at the beginning of Islam, then they multiplied and became strong (واستحكموا), so their affair advanced in a way that delighted people. ** لِيَغِيظَ بِهِمُ الْكُفَّارَ ** (that He may enrage the faithless by them)—this is the reason (علّة) for likening them to the crop in its growth (زكائه) and strength (واستحكامه). وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ مِنْهُمْ مَغْفِرَةً وَأَجْراً عَظِيماً.** (Allah has promised those of them who have faith and do righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward.)

In al-Amali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked concerning whom this verse was revealed. He said: "When the Day of Resurrection comes, a banner (لواء) of the brightest light (أنور) will be tied, and a caller will cry out: 'Let the Master of the Faithful (سيّد المؤمنين) stand up, and with him those who believed, and Allah has sent Muhammad.' So Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both) will stand up, and Allah will give the banner of white light into His hand. Beneath it will be all the foremost early believers (السابقين الأوّلين) from the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار); no one else will mix with them, until he sits upon a pulpit (منبر) of the light of the Lord of Glory (ربّ العزّة). And all of them will be presented to him, man by man, and he will be given his reward (اجره) and his light (نوره). When the last of them has passed, it will be said to them: 'You have known your place and your stations in Paradise. Indeed, your Lord says to you: "I have for you forgiveness and a great reward," meaning Paradise.' Then Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) will stand up, and the people beneath his banner will be with him until he enters Paradise. Then he will return to his pulpit and will continue to present all the faithful to him, taking his share of them into Paradise and leaving some groups (اقواماً) for the Fire." (The rest of the hadith is omitted).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Protect your wealth, your women, and what your right hands possess from ruin by reciting ** إِنَّا فَتَحْنا لَكَ فَتْحاً ** (Indeed, We have granted you a manifest victory). For if one is among those who constantly recite it, a caller will cry out on the Day of Resurrection, so that all creatures hear: 'You are one of My sincere servants (عبادي المخلصين). Join him with the righteous among My servants, settle him in the Gardens of Bliss (جنّات النعيم), and give him to drink from the sealed wine (الرحيق المختوم) mixed with camphor (الكافور)."

footnote (1) Meaning their sign (*علامتهم*) on the Day of Resurrection is that the places of their prostration will be intensely white.
